Arc Infrastructure manages and develops transport infrastructure assets that support growth and create jobs in WA. Rail is at the heart of our business, and the 5,500km rail network is the backbone of freight transport in Western Australia from the diverse Midwest across to the fascinating Goldfields and Yilgarn regions through to the stunning South West and Great Southern. We’re committed to working with industry, our customers, partners and communities to find new opportunities that will support and strengthen our rail network for the benefit of WA.



The Opportunity
 
We are seeking a highly analytical professional with strong financial modelling capabilities to support investment analysis and corporate decision‑making to join our team in the role of Senior Business Development Analyst based near Redcliffe Station / Perth Airport.
 
The Role

You will be responsible for developing and growing Arc Infrastructure in line with strategic objectives through the identification and development of new customers, tasks and infrastructure investments.

The main role responsibilities are to:

Develop and manage detailed financial models including scenario and sensitivity analysis to assess investment opportunities.
Develop new and complimentary rail, transport and other infrastructure related opportunities consistent with the commercial, investment and strategic objectives of Arc.
Establish and maintain relationships with existing and potential rail customers and assist them in establishing rail-based solutions.
Work with Arc internal teams (especially Commercial, Legal, Engineering and Operations) to progress new rail and other infrastructure opportunities through the investment stage gates from identification to operation.
Manage in-flight investment projects through full project lifecycle and extending to the development of approval of all business case funding requests
Develop strong commercial relationships with key government agencies, relevant commercial entities and industry bodies where these relationships support arc infrastructure rail related business development initiatives.  
 
About You

To be successful in this role you will need to have:

Advanced Excel modelling skills, including dynamic formulas, scenario tools, and model structuring best practices
Ability to build investment‑focused models and run corporate models
Experience in managing commercial and strategic projects
Experience in preparing business cases and funding submissions
Degree Qualification in Business or Commerce or equivalent work experience
Infrastructure, mining or transport industry background preferred
5-10 years commercial experience

We promote a work environment that is characterised by personal accountability, mutual trust and respect and a genuine interest in employees.  We understand our employees are the key to our success and as a team, our focus is to ensure they feel safe, valued and fulfilled in the work that they do. Establish integration standards, governance frameworks and best-practice development processes

Partner with business leaders and vendors to deliver secure, reliable and fit-for-purpose solutions

What You Bring


7+ years' experience in application development, enterprise integrations or digital platform roles
Proven hands-on experience with Microsoft 365, Power Platform, Dynamics 365 or comparable SaaS environments
Strong technical depth in automation tools and enterprise integration platforms (e.g. Azure Integration Services, Boomi, MuleSoft)
Deep understanding of REST APIs, event-driven architecture, middleware and enterprise integration patterns
Demonstrated experience working with foundation AI models such as OpenAI GPT, Anthropic Claude or Google Gemini including practical application in an enterprise context
A track record of translating business needs into secure, scalable and future-ready digital solutions
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence across technical and non-technical audiences
A collaborative mindset and the confidence to lead teams through change

What We Offer


We offer the opportunity to work for a multinational company with real scope to take your career to the next level. Benefits include but not limited to:

Free parking and gym facilities at our Macquarie Park office
Bonus leave including Birthday leave, Canon Day leave and Kyosei Day

Ad Text:
Do work that makes a difference!


Contribute to nationally significant projects supporting Australia's real‑time payments systems

Develop your career in a collaborative, high‑performing environment

Access extensive staff benefits, including on‑site gym facilities, fitness classes and canteen


About the Role/Team

The Payments Settlements (PS) department is seeking a data-oriented Lead Business Analyst to uplift, modernise and maintain the data warehouse supporting critical payments systems-including the Reserve Bank Information and Transfer System (RITS), the Fast Settlement Service (FSS), and SWIFT interbank payment systems.

You will be a key member of the Business Delivery section and will work as an important bridge between business and technology, ensuring requirements are clearly defined and aligned for the successful delivery of the projects that support/ enhance the Australian payments system. This role will initially sit with the FSS Next Program, a major 3-5‑year transformation initiative that will enhance real‑time, 24/7 settlement capabilities for the New Payments Platform (NPP), which processes an average of 3.5 million payments per day, worth $4 billion. In this program, you will evaluate various data platform options, contribute to business case development, and help deliver a new technology solution capable of managing large‑scale transactional datasets.

What you will do

Engage with business owners, SMEs and technical teams to elicit, interpret and document business requirements

Identify and map data assets & data products, end-to-end processes, data lineage, ownership and map business workflows to data flows.

Facilitate structured workshops for discovery, ideation and requirements gathering for the new data platform.

Collaborate with data analysts, architects, developers and design teams to convert business needs into functional specifications for scalable data warehouse and technology solutions.

Support data modelling and data preparation for downstream analytics

Create and maintain comprehensive documentation, including user stories for Agile delivery.

Prepare and present high-quality decision support materials, including options and trade-off analysis, to senior leadership and executive stakeholders

Identify risks, interdependencies, and cross-functional impacts across business and technology domains

Manage requirement changes using structured change‑control processes and maintain traceability.

Contribute to change‑management planning and execution.

Contribute to the enhancement of business analysis practice, tools, and templates, and mentor junior analysts.


Your background

To be successful in this critical role you will possess:

Advanced knowledge of business analysis methods, elicitation techniques and documentation standards

Strong proficiency with JIRA, Confluence, Azure DevOps or similar tools

Experience in data platforms, data‑transformation or modernisation programs

Solid understanding of data assets, data products, data lineage and transformation techniques

Experience with structured and semi‑structured datasets and data engineering delivery practices

Strong process mapping and workflow design skills

Deep knowledge of data warehouse architecture, ETL processes and data modelling

A "technical BA" mindset, comfortable working with database schemas and ERDs

Strong SQL skills for data analysis, data mapping, extraction and profiling

Excellent analytical, multi-tasking, organizational and stakeholder management skills.

Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst in data-centric environments or projects, with a strong focus on data warehousing, analytics, and/or enterprise transformation initiatives

A professional certification in business analysis such as IIBA CBAP would be highly regarded.



Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

ASIC's Data, Analytics and AI (AiDA) team is seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join their team. The successful candidate will provide comprehensive leadership in relation to data, insights, machine learning, and AI systems whin ASIC.

24 Month Fixed Term Position
Salary from $152,074 to $181,284 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % Superannuation
Applications close at 11:59pm AEDT, Thursday 26 March 2026

 

The team 

The Data, Analytics and AI (AiDA) team is essential for ASIC's transformation into a digitally enabled, data-informed regulator. The data science team within AiDA collaborates with business units to develop machine learning solutions, utilizing the latest technologies to address regulatory and compliance challenges in surveillance and enforcement. 

 

The role

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be central to data, insights, machine learning, and AI. You will provide best practices, lead, and deliver high-impact machine learning and AI systems or products with stakeholders. Additionally, you will conduct experiments and assess evolving trends, emerging risks, and systemic misconduct in financial services and markets.

More specifically, you will: 

Present insights and learnings to key stakeholders and executives, driving accountability for action and impact

Lead AI/ML solutions or products from initial brief to implementation, managing stakeholder expectations for timely delivery and usage
Design and improve operational processes for data collection, AI/ML model deployment and risk identification
Coordinate and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to design and develop standardised, transferrable and reusable frameworks and architectures for AI/ ML solutions across ASIC

 

About you

The ideal candidate should have a strong background in data, insights, machine learning, and AI. They must excel in delivering high-impact AI/ML systems and providing best practices. Proficiency in data analysis, statistical methods, modelling, data visualization and storytelling is essential. Additionally, they should be skilled in designing and improving processes for data collection, AI/ML model deployment, and risk identification. Establishing and promoting MLOps capabilities for consistent ML workloads across ASIC is crucial.

 

In addition, you will have:

Tertiary qualifications in a quantitative discipline such as Applied Finance, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Economics
5+ years of industry experience in AI, Machine Learning Engineering, Data Science
Demonstrated advanced level of proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, SQL
Previous experience using data science machine learning platforms like Amazon SageMaker, Azure ML, Databricks, and Vertex AI
Experience in manipulating and analysing complex, semi-structured, or unstructured data to solve challenging problems
Experience in areas such as Generative AI, RAG Architecture, PySpark or Graph Analytics is advantageous
Strong communication skills with the ability to present insights to stakeholders and provide thought leadership at all levels
Proficiency in risk management strategies to identify, mitigate, and manage data or privacy related risks

Provide strategic support to the Executive Director, Natural Ecosystems (NE) to shape and advance the NE portfolio, contributing to strategic planning, prioritisation and portfolio development aligned with Minderoo's 2030 Strategy.

Design and facilitate strategic planning and learning workshops to support alignment, prioritisation and adaptive decision-making across the portfolio.

Coordinate portfolio oversight and governance processes, including work planning, project approval processes and internal progress reporting.

Support financial planning and portfolio management, including budgeting, reforecasting and resource planning in collaboration with FP&A.

Leverage impact data, evidence and insights to inform strategy and portfolio adaptation, enabling teams to identify opportunities, manage risks and improve outcomes.

Promote evaluative thinking and continuous learning across the organisation, embedding learning and adaptation into strategy development and program implementation.

Translate insights into compelling reporting and strategic storytelling for internal and external stakeholders, supporting informed decision-making and building trust and credibility.

Collaborate across Minderoo and the broader sector, identifying opportunities for alignment, innovation and shared learning while staying abreast of emerging trends and best practice in systems-led strategy and learning.

Attractive Parental Leave
An inclusive, values-driven workplace

Ad Text:
Drive transformation with your D365 Finance expertise and join our team today
 
 
 
Role Summary
 
 
If you're ready to build a serious career in Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, this is the role to do it in. We're looking for an emerging D365 FO Functional Consultant to join our Supply Tech Finance team, a close-knit group working at the intersection of ERP technology and complex travel finance operations.
 

This is a hands-on, learning-forward role where you'll support the configuration, implementation, and ongoing optimisation of D365 FinOps across modules including General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and financial reporting. You'll work alongside experienced consultants, Finance Product Managers, and a cross-functional Engineering team spanning Australia and India — gaining real project exposure in a genuinely supportive environment.

 

 

What You'll Do
Configure and support D365 FinOps modules, including GL, AP, AR, and financial workflows with guidance from senior consultants
Document business requirements, functional specifications, and process maps to support Finance system changes and enhancements
Assist with the implementation, testing, and quality assurance of D365 FinOps solutions and module configurations
Support end-user training, change management activities, and post-go-live hypercare
Help troubleshoot functional issues arising from version upgrades, configuration changes, or integration events
Maintain project documentation in Confluence and manage tasks in Jira across the FCTG Atlassian space
Collaborate with Business Analysts, Developers, Architects, and Finance stakeholders to deliver effective solutions

 

 

Preferred Qualifications
Do you hold a Certificate in MB310 (highly regarded)
Hold a Bachelor of Accounting degree, which is preferred. Or a Finance degree
1–2 years' experience with ERP systems ideally Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations or similar platforms (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite)
Foundational understanding of core finance processes such as Accounts Payable, General Ledger, or Procure-to-Pay
Strong desire to build deep D365 FinOps functional expertise within a complex, real-world environment
Clear communicator with the ability to work effectively across Finance, Engineering, and Product teams
Knowledge of Power Platform, Azure, or data migration concepts is a bonus

 

 

Your Day to Day

You'll gain hands-on exposure to a live D365 FinOps environment supporting a global travel company — with real project ownership from day one. You'll be surrounded by experienced mentors, a collaborative cross-functional team, and a clear pathway to grow into a highly capable D365 FO specialist.

 

 

What you'll enjoy:

 

🙋🏾‍♀️Culture: you will be employed by an inclusive company that offers a team culture like no other in the industry - Flight Centre Travel Group Australia is proud to be a certified Great Place To Work® Australia New Zealand Pty Ltd

🌏Travel Discounts: Including family and friends - flights, hotels, tours, cruises, travel insurance and more
 
💎Brightness of Future: Career opportunities in a network of brands and businesses across the globe - we promote from within

Job description:



What you'll be doing

A Principal Consultant wears many hats - you are not only technically credible across a wider range of technologies, practices and disciplines, but you are also the translation engine for our customers. Working both on new engagements and inflight projects you will provide guidance to the delivery teams, seek out new opportunities and assist the Sales team in analysing client's requirements and crafting solutions to address their desired business outcomes.

• 80% of your time will be spent on Project Delivery
• 10% of your time will be spent on Sales Support
• 10% of your time will be spent on IP Creation

You will be involved with clients from both a technical and mentoring perspective, partnering with them to not only uplift their environment but to upskill them as well. This is a great opportunity if you want to continue to learn and develop alongside some of the best minds in the industry working across challenging projects.

You will be as comfortable in the sales and concept phase of the customer engagement as you are in the ongoing evolution of the client relationship with a specific focus on innovation and governance and as a trusted partner. You'll be a pivotal role in knowledge sharing internally within the business, providing leadership oversight and governing multiple delivery engagements, assisting and mentoring other leaders and squad members in their assigned projects and at every opportunity, ensuring team members have exposure to your abilities.

Key Responsibilities

Lead, coach, support and empower the consulting team to do their jobs to the best of their ability without the need to rely on a hierarchy.
Be able to build trusted relationships at all levels
Design, implement, and maintain complex integration solutions across various systems and applications.
Define integration strategy across various integration platforms, API's and event-driven services.
Lead and contribute to solution design discussions
Lead or contribute to code reviews and technical design/specifications
Provide architecture governance and solution assurance.
Contribute to the enhancement of in-house delivery methodologies
Contribute to the review and analysis of business requirements
Perform technical assessments and sizing.
Contribute to the delivery process by being part of client meetings, making presentations, and at times working as part of the tender response team.
Create, maintain, and present technical documentation to different audiences at different levels of seniority and technical expertise
Provide strategic technology guidance and keep in touch with current trends in latest methodologies, including delivering ‘Lunch & Learn' sessions.
Mentoring junior developers and contribute to best practices, code reviews, and documentation.

Working Conditions

Full time permanent opportunity
Based in Brisbane

What will make you succesful?

The position necessitates a highly motivated individual who can provide strong technical skills, along with leadership, guidance, and coaching abilities, to deliver quality focused outcomes.

Strong knowledge of middleware, ETL, cloud and data integration concepts
Hands-on experience with Boomi iPaaS and Azure Integration Services
Strong understanding of API Management and Master Data Management
A mindset for innovation and a passion for optimizing processes
Experience in integrating with core enterprise software applications (both OnPrem and in the Cloud), including applications such as Netsuite, Salesforce, Oracle, SAP, Workday, TechnologyOne, Dayforce etc.
Experience with business and technical requirement analysis, business process modeling/mapping, methodology development, and data mapping;
Strong understanding of enterprise application integration patterns;
Strong communication (written/verbal) skills;
Demonstrated ability to contribute and self-manage within a small team environment;
Expertise in the assessment, architectural design, and implementation of integration patterns including Bulk Data, Real-Time, File Based, Cloud, and Event Processing;
Expertise with B2B integration concepts, standards, architectures; and
Knowledge of relational database design and data management techniques

Why is this an exciting opportunity?

Atturra is one of the fastest growing ASX-listed advisory and IT solutions companies in Australia, giving you a strong foundation to fulfil your career ambitions.

We currently have 1100+ professionals working across Australia, New Zealand, Singapore and Hong Kong who are using innovation to lead the way.

The Data & Integration business unit helps clients unlock the full potential of data by integrating systems and applications, enabling digital transformation through our comprehensive on-premises, hybrid, and cloud integration solutions. We specialise in Boomi, Smartsheet, and OpenText.

What our people say:

"Atturra has a great working environment and management are very supportive of their staff. We celebrate success and have fun while working hard."

Why you'll love working here

Culture of possibility

At Atturra we embrace innovation within our teams, fostering an environment of possibility and growth. We believe in creating a supportive and welcoming culture, where everyone is encouraged to achieve success through collaboration, hard work and dedication.

A strong set of company values

Our culture is reflected by our employees who demonstrate camaraderie, innovation, dedication, respect and excellence in everything they do. These traits have become integral to the way we do business and are the values we strive for.

Valuable employee benefits

Career growth via succession planning, internal mobility programs, and mentorship opportunities.
Ongoing investment in professional development through industry certifications.
Employee benefits, recognition, and wellbeing platform.Mental health support through our Employee Assistance Program.
Support for family and caring responsibilities, including paid parental leave.
Employee referral program, with monetary incentives offered.

Commitment to supporting Australia's veterans

Atturra is proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Department of Veterans Affairs' Veteran Employment Commitment (VEC) and is committed to supporting Australia's veteran community. We aim to enhance the employment experience for veterans by improving recruitment, support, retention, and leadership opportunities to ensure they have a meaningful and impactful career with us.

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst - Canberra or Sydney - Hybrid
Ad Text:
Your new role

The Senior Finance Analyst plays a key role in supporting CSC’s strategic and operational objectives by delivering high‑quality financial analysis, reporting and insight across the organisation. Working closely with the Finance Business Partners, you will provide timely, accurate and practical insights that enable confident decision‑making across business, projects and major transformation initiatives.

Analytically strong and naturally curious, you will enjoy asking the right questions and translating complex data into meaningful insights. You will provide dedicated analytical capability to support portfolio level cost transparency, improve forecasting accuracy and drive proactive financial management. As a trusted team player with strong problem solving skills, you will also contribute to broader Finance Business Partnering activities by providing analytical support across portfolios as required.

Key responsibilities:

Prepare and analyse financial reporting for spend across BAU, projects and transformation programs, ensuring outputs are accurate, timely and clearly explained.
Support the preparation of forecasts, scenarios and multi-year financial plans, contributing to improved accuracy and forward looking insight.
Analyse cost drivers, trends and variances, proactively identifying emerging risks, pressures and opportunities for cost management and optimisation.
Build, maintain and enhance cost models to support funding proposals, business cases, benefit tracking and Executive decision papers.
Assist with month end and budget processes, including review of coding accuracy, reconciliations, accruals, capitalisation and project allocations.
Work collaboratively with the Finance Business Partners and the broader Finance team to improve data quality, reporting processes and analytical capability.

What you’ll need to excel

Degree from a recognised tertiary institution in Finance, Accounting or Commerce
Appropriate professional qualification (e.g. CA, CPA or equivalent), or a relevant non-accounting qualification (e.g. an applicable MBA) is desirable
Experience with large financial management information systems (e.g. Technology One, SAP, Oracle, etc.) is desirable
Strong commercial acumen with proven ability to partner and influence at Executive level
Clear, confident communicator with experience providing practical, data‑driven insights
Experience delivering high‑quality reporting for senior executives and/or Boards
High attention to detail with strong numerical and analytical capability
Solid experience in budgeting, forecasting and variance analysis
Background in financial services and/or project environments
High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office suite, including Outlook, Word, and PowerPoint, with proficiency in Excel

Why join CSC?

A permanent full-time role which offers hybrid working arrangements from our Sydney or Canberra office.
Join high‑performing, inclusive team where development, growth and progression are actively encouraged. Leaders are invested in your success and create a culture of opportunity.
Be part of a collaborative team committed to enhancing the quality of reporting and analysis through a mindset of continuous improvement, growing alongside the function as it evolves.

How to apply

To apply for this opportunity, please submit your CV via the Apply Link in this advertisement.

To ensure all applications are considered in the review process, we request you to please apply through the advertised links only.

Applications close: 7 April 2026

For any other questions about this role, contact recruitment@csc.gov.au
Agency submissions will not be accepted.

What you’ll get in return

At CSC, we’re committed to more than super — we’re committed to people. That means creating a workplace that values diversity, promotes equity, and fosters inclusion across everything we do.

Our gender equality strategy is more than a promise — it’s a plan. We're actively increasing representation in our workforce, removing barriers to career progression, and creating pathways for all employees to grow and succeed.

We welcome candidates of all genders, cultural backgrounds, ages, sexualities, and abilities. We offer flexible work, inclusive policies, and development opportunities designed to support you at every stage of your career.

Position Description: Energy Market Analyst

Location: Brisbane, Queensland

Reports to: Director, Networks

Company Overview:

Blackstart is a dynamic and growing professional services firm recognised for its expertise in navigating the technical, commercial, and regulatory complexities of the energy transition. Our work spans the Australian and South-East Asian energy markets, supporting high impact projects across generation, transmission, storage, and large energy users. We pride ourselves on a collaborative environment, meaningful professional development opportunities, and a commitment to innovation.

Role Overview:

As an Energy Market Analyst, you will play a central role in modelling, analysing, and interpreting energy market dynamics to support client projects and strategic decision making. You will develop and run electricity market models, assess project economics, analyse market scenarios, and contribute to commercial and regulatory insights.

Key Responsibilities:

Energy Market Modelling & Analysis:

- Build, calibrate, and run energy market models to assess dispatch outcomes, price forecasts, and market behaviour.

- Develop long term market scenarios including sensitivities.

- Analyse results to support project feasibility, investment cases, network connection strategies, and commercial assessments.

- Interpret model outputs into insights suitable for technical and executive audiences.

Commercial & Financial Insight:

- Support project valuation and commercial modelling across renewable, storage, hydrogen, and hybrid assets.

- Assess revenue streams such as arbitrage, FCAS, capacity products, PPAs, and emerging markets.

- Identify market risks and opportunities across investment horizons.

Regulatory & Policy Analysis:

- Monitor regulatory developments across the NEM and WEM

- Assess impacts of policy and rule changes on client strategy and market outcomes.

- Contribute to submissions and strategy papers.

Reporting & Communication:

- Prepare high‑quality reports, presentations, dashboards, and visualisations.

- Present findings to clients and internal stakeholders.

- Communicate complex modelling concepts in clear, plain language.

Client Engagement & Collaboration:

- Work with clients to understand project needs and modelling assumptions.

- Collaborate with technical specialists, and commercial analysts.

- Support proposal development and study delivery.

Innovation & Continuous Improvement:

- Recommend improvements to modelling workflows and datasets.

- Stay up to date with emerging technologies and market frameworks.

- Support internal capability building.

Qualifications & Experience:

Education:

- Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Economics, Mathematics, Data Science, or related field; a Master's degree is highly desirable.

Technical & Analytical Skills:

- Strong quantitative skills with experience in market or power system modelling.

- Experience with PLEXOS, PSO, Aurora, BID3, or similar tools. Experience with PLEXOS is highly advantageous.

- Proficiency in Python, R, VBA, and SQL desirable.

Industry Experience:

- 2–6 years in electricity markets, energy economics, market modelling, or similar fields.

- Prior energy sector experience is a requirement for the role.

- Familiarity with NEM structure and regulatory frameworks.

- Working knowledge of Australian energy regulatory requirements is required for the role.

Communication & Collaboration:

- Excellent written and verbal communication.

- Ability to manage competing priorities and work in multidisciplinary teams.

Why Blackstart?

At Blackstart you will use your hard-earned skills to make a meaningful impact on the real-world challenges that need to be overcome to enable the energy transition. Blackstart are committed to sustainable personal and professional growth and believe that business objectives must be harmonised with this growth.

Career Growth: Opportunities to work on high-impact projects that shape the energy sector in Australia.

Relocation Support: Relocation support packages for candidates moving from overseas, including visa assistance and support with settling in Australia.

Balance: Flexible working arrangements and a commitment to maintaining a healthy balance to achieve your personal goals as well as those of Blackstart.

Healthy Work Environment: Work in a healthy and inclusive environment that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.



The Billing and Credit function, part of Group Finance and Corporate, oversees billing, payments, collections, recoveries, credit, and fraud risk management across Optus. As a Senior Data Scientist within this team, you will leverage advanced analytics and machine learning to deliver timely, accurate, and actionable insights that help identify emerging risks, strengthen fraud and credit controls, and reduce financial loss—ensuring a seamless billing and payments experience for Optus customers.

This role requires a strong mix of technical expertise and business acumen, applying tools such as Databricks, SQL, Python, or R to design, deploy, and refine predictive models and scorecards. You will extract and analyse large datasets, develop innovative risk solutions, and present insights through compelling visualizations to guide decision-making. With experience in credit, fraud, and collections, you'll play a critical role in shaping strategies that protect revenue, manage risk, and support sustainable growth across Consumer, Small Business, Mid-Market, Enterprise, and Wholesale segments.

What You'll Do

Lead delivery of advanced data models and analytics driving all Credit and Fraud Risk decisions across Optus Group.
Design and implement strategy changes to manage 3M+ applications annually through automated decisioning within SLA.
Oversee backend system development and reporting tools (Tableau, Cognos, IDW/EDW, Credit systems, SAS/R models).
Partner with senior stakeholders and business units to enhance financial performance and implement key initiatives.
Plan, track, and coordinate performance targets, transformation priorities, and strategic reviews across Group Credit & Finance.

What makes you perfect for the role?

BA/BS degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Analytics, or equivalent applied quantitative field
Advanced data analysis, SQL, Python, R, Databricks; strong financial and predictive modelling; ML algorithms applied to business problems.
5+ years in data science/finance roles with telco expertise; credit, fraud, and collections risk management; commercial and regulatory understanding.
Proven ability to build business cases, conduct financial modelling, and translate complex data into actionable recommendations.
Skilled at influencing and presenting to senior management; experience leading initiatives across Product, Sales, Commercial, and Corporate functions.
Strong presentation, negotiation, and interpersonal skills; ability to manage diverse stakeholders and drive cross-functional projects.

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.

This includes more than 200 projects such as the Metro Tunnel, North East Link, West Gate Tunnel, removing level crossings, new and upgraded hospitals in Footscray, Frankston and Warrnambool, and specialist programs.



Job description:



About VIDA Health

VIDA Health (Victorian Health Building Authority) is responsible for the rollout of the Victorian Government's multi-billion-dollar health capital projects. We're delivering world-class health facilities across Victoria including new public hospitals and emergency care, community health services, ambulance branches, residential aged care and mental health facilities. VIDA Health also provides technical support across engineering, property, asset management and environmental sustainability disciplines.

The Opportunity

As Manager, Project Data Governance, you will lead a team of analysts within the Project Controls branch to ensure project data is accurate, consistent, well-governed and fit for purpose. Your work will underpin critical internal and external reporting to senior executives, Ministers, central agencies and the public.

This is a senior leadership role focused on establishing strong data governance frameworks, driving quality and assurance across project reporting, and enabling meaningful insights through modern analytics and visualisation tools.

Working closely with project teams, Reporting & Analytics, and key stakeholders across VIDA and government, you will ensure project data supports informed decision-making across the full project lifecycle.


Key aspects of the role include:

AEMO at the Heart of Energy

 AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this.

We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection.

 Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector!

 About the Team & the Position

 The System Design Division plays a critical role in AEMO’s success, driving the organisation’s collaborative and industry-wide efforts in meeting the challenges of a transitioning energy system. The Division includes specialist areas in system planning, forecasting, future energy systems as well as onboarding and connections.

 The Market Operability team is seeking a Principal Analyst to undertake advanced energy modelling and analysis for the east coast gas and electricity systems. The team is responsible for the delivery of Gas Statement of Opportunities (GSOO), operability forecasting for the Integrated System Plan (ISP) for the National Electricity Market (NEM) and integrated gas and electricity modelling for the ISP.

 As a Principal Analyst, you will be responsible for providing strategic direction to key reports, overseeing modelling and analysis, and communicating results to various internal and external stakeholders. You will use a mix of forecast methods for the gas and electricity markets and modelling techniques focused on system optimisation over the short, medium and long-term. The Principal Analyst will lead model developments and improvements affecting short-term modelling to better analyse the reliability of the future energy system and support the development of the ISP

 Position Responsibilities:

Provide expert technical advice, expertise, and leadership on market modelling matters, including coordination of the GSOO process, leading short-term market modelling components of the ISP.

Apply advanced modelling methods, economic theory, and/or technical engineering knowledge to finalise model inputs and assumptions, run models, analyse results, and present analysis in written reports and verbal presentations.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

Support the Manager in people-management related processes, such as recruitment and development of staff, training, and regular feedback and performance appraisals.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

 Position Requirements

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in energy market simulation software (preferably PLEXOS) and forecasting tools and processes.

Demonstrated experience in energy market analysis (electricity and/or gas) and the interpretation of analysis performed by others.

Demonstrable experience in managing projects and people to achieve timely delivery of specified outcomes.

Knowledge and experience in analysing the physical operation of the electricity and/or gas systems and the overarching markets, and

understanding of power system reliability assessments.

Experience in writing technical reports and other documents that convey, in simple terms, complex ideas and analysis.

Very strong communication skills, particularly the ability to present modelling and analysis, and make recommendations to internal and external stakeholders

Education/ professional Certifications: 

Tertiary and/or post graduate qualifications in mathematics, economics, data science, physics or engineering or an appropriate technology discipline.
